data.text: $BRK.B Following up from previous mind. It's been a while, but as expected this has been floating around that level we marked out from last year. Did not push to previous highs around $540 and instead pushed towards that secondary local high around $520 before falling back into consolidation. This will likely continue to consolidate this year. A local low could form as early as May and as late as October before rallying and pushing towards ATHs. All depends on the status of the overall economy/market, in regard to recession, geopolitical status, etc. (all unknowns). But general picture is ATHs within the next two years. Patience is the name of the game. Will follow up later this year

$BRK.B Berkshire Hathaway’s latest quarter, Warren Buffett’s final earnings call as CEO, showed steady core business performance, a strong insurance boost, and growing cash reserves. Operating earnings rose to $14.4 billion, revenue increased 2% to $95 billion, and net earnings climbed 17% to $30.9 billion. Insurance underwriting more than tripled to $2.4 billion, while BNSF and manufacturing posted modest gains. The balance sheet hit a record $382 billion in cash and equivalents, though the company continued to avoid share buybacks. 

Despite lagging stock performance since Buffett’s retirement announcement, the $9.7 billion OxyChem acquisition signals Abel’s first major move and sets the stage for future capital deployment. Overall, the quarter reflects solid underlying results, a strong financial position, and a careful transition to new leadership.

$BRK.B is sitting in a textbook Qullamaggie breakout in a bullish flag on the 1H chart. It is sitting on support levels from 8/27, 9/10, 9/16, 10/10, 10/16 and if you happen to scroll back a bit to 4/9. We are also sitting at the April TACO V shape recovery support. When the world zigs, do we zag? Same with game theory tit for tat etc. If you look up the option chain, there is very little resistance. We ride at dawn gents and gawns.

$BRK.B Undervalued Amid Steady Growth Buffett's Long-Term Play?

$BRK.A 
What Could Shine / Competitive Advantages

Massive cash reserves give Berkshire a position of strength during market downturns — they can buy assets at “value” prices when others are forced to sell.

Diversification across insurance, railroads (BNSF), utilities and energy (BHE), retail, and manufacturing provides multiple pillars to withstand sector-specific shocks.

Core operations remain profitable in stable conditions, adding resilience. For example, Geico improving its underwriting performance is a positive signal.

Greg Abel is already active in key operational roles, which should smooth the leadership transition and reduce the sense of a sudden leap into uncertainty.

https://es.tradingview.com/chart/BRK.A/LLgX7cYk/

$BRK.B 📉 Fire Powell again ? Fight with Elon. Is this economic leadership or personal chaos?

Trump’s recent statement about firing Powell again —
combined with his public attacks on Elon Musk —
raises deeper questions than just policy.

It’s about whether the next leader of the U.S. can separate ego from economics.

🇺🇸 The world watches U.S. markets because of credibility and structure.
If Fed independence collapses…
If capital investment is politicized…
If innovation leaders like Elon are targeted for personal reasons…

What’s left?
Markets don’t just price earnings. They price stability.

Maybe it’s time we stop asking, “Will markets go up?”
And start asking, “Is this the kind of leadership markets can even trust?”

#SPX #BTC #Trump #Powell #ElonMusk #MacroRisk #Fed #InstitutionalTrust #FirePowell #TradingView

$SPY $SPX Good morning traders! All major holdings of SPY are significantly losing money, with $TSLA being down more than 8%, which indicates strong selling pressure in the market today. Other stocks, such as $AAPL, $NVDA, $META, and $AVGO, have also lost money remarkably in the past two hours. Meanwhile, $BRK.B is the only stock showing bullish momentum today. The key level for today is 575, and since we are currently down almost 10 points from this level, buying calls on 0DTE options may be considered a safer play. However, I do not recommend risking a large amount of money on calls today due to the strong selling pressure (ROC = -1.34). There is also a high probability that designated market makers may fail to push the price to the maximum pain level. Currently, the price is trading in the discount zone and is below all major exponential moving averages. Pay close attention to price action, as we will be placing our first order very soon. Comments and suggestions?

$NDQ $SPX $BRK.B $AAPL $BTCUSDT The only reason Berkshire Hathaway outperformed S&P 500 is because of its Apple holdings. This is the first time Warren Buffet not doing buybacks. I’m not sure whether he thinks the market is just overvalued somehow so he’s keep buying short term bonds and holding cash readying for future correction. He lost lots of possible profits gains by selling TSMC or due to Charlie Munger’s death who had been equally contrarian of Warren Buffets conservative strategies.

$SPX $BTCUSDT $NAS100 $AAPL $BRKB Warren buffet sold stocks Q3 again. He’s a wealthy investor who made billions by investing during favorable economic times, likely due to his early exposure to finance. Buffett has been consistently selling Apple shares recently. However, he may be either too old to understand current technological revolutions or simply overrated. He missed the mark with investments like TSMC, Kraft Heinz, and Occidental Petroleum and owns few tech-heavy assets, aside from Apple. He continued to sell stocks in Q2 even as the market rallied, and recent reports indicate that he sold more of Apple and Bank of America in Q3, with Berkshire Hathaway holding $325.5 billion in cash. He maybe wrong again but it’s worth noting.
